2026-03-23T03:00:40.2145162Z 
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2145511Z You are in 'detached HEAD' state. You can look around, make experimental
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2146279Z changes and commit them, and you can discard any commits you make in this
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2147092Z state without impacting any branches by switching back to a branch.
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2147500Z 
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2147815Z If you want to create a new branch to retain commits you create, you may
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2148561Z do so (now or later) by using -c with the switch command. Example:
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2148967Z 
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2149133Z   git switch -c <new-branch-name>
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2149407Z 
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2149570Z Or undo this operation with:
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2149804Z 
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2149885Z   git switch -
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2150004Z 
2026-03-23T03:00:40.2150235Z Turn off this advice by setting config variable advice.detachedHead to false
